mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of the Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE), a managed Kubernetes service that integrates seamlessly with Vultr's cloud infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a straightforward way to deploy and manage Kubernetes clusters without the overhead of manual setup. Key trade-offs include reliance on Vultr's infrastructure and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Vultr command-line interface, which is necessary for managing your Vultr resources.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr-cli /usr/local/bin/

vultr-cli version

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH
> Verify the installation by checking the version

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --region 'ewr' --version '1.30' --node-count 3 --plan 'vc2-2c-4gb'

```

> Replace 'ewr' with your preferred region

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> > ~/.kube/config

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler-vultr.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/main/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the cluster autoscaler
> Deploy monitoring tools

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete --cluster-id <your-cluster-id>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

vultr-cli loadbalancer delete --all

vultr-cli volumes delete --all

```

> Delete all load balancers
> Delete all orphaned volumes

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current state of your nodes.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

vultr-cli k8s upgrade --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> --version '1.31'

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of nodes before upgrade
> Upgrade to the latest version
> Verify the nodes are in 'Ready' status after upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or cluster certificate.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the kubeconfig file using 'vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <your-cluster-id>'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not properly initialized or resources are not deployed.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your resources are deployed and check the namespace with 'kubectl get all --all-namespaces'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your nodes or check for resource limits on existing pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the cloud provider settings are misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify your Vultr account settings and ensure that the API key is correct.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Vultr regions and improved load balancer integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy API keys; use the new API key format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.29, ensure that your API keys are updated to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
